claim: Verifier confirms that gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness: explorer and cross-checker independently verified the boundary audit implementation through code inspection, test coverage review, and artifact analysis, with no contradictions found and all assumptions properly declared.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() verified by both agents: git ls-files + walkFiles() for sha256 digests, allowed_write_roots excluded via isWithin() check.
evidence.why: Core boundary mechanism independently verified by both agents with consistent findings.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot timing and blocker creation verified: pre-execution baseline at line 516, post-execution audit at lines 566-572, BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers created on changes.
evidence.why: Verification that boundary enforcement creates blockers as claimed.

evidence.summary: execution_policy verified by both agents: project_mutation='forbidden', allowed_write_roots restricted to artifact directory, agent_instruction='boundary_controlled'.
evidence.why: Policy contract that governs evidence agent behavior independently confirmed.

evidence.summary: Claude-specific boundary settings independently verified: claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='', claude_disallowed_tools='', claude_no_session_persistence=true, agent_tool_profile normalization.
evidence.why: Provider-specific tool boundary implementation cross-checked successfully.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() independently verified: validates project-scoped evidence includes repo-relative path, returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers when evidence lacks project sources.
evidence.why: Validation logic that blocks PRD readiness for missing project evidence confirmed by both agents.

evidence.summary: Test coverage for boundary enforcement independently verified: mutation blocking (381-414), untracked file detection (416-452), artifact exclusion (493-533). All tests match explorer descriptions.
evidence.why: Executable evidence that boundary enforcement works as tested.

evidence.summary: Real dispatch artifact independently verified: boundary.project_mutation='clean', readiness.blocked=true due to evidence issues (EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING, EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT), not mutations.
evidence.why: Real-world evidence that boundary audit succeeded and PRD stayed blocked on evidence gaps, not mutations.

evidence.summary: Stress test infrastructure independently verified: 'local-contract-boundary' scenario matches current demand, 7 scenarios defined including 'git-untracked-boundary', 'json-output-resilience', 'provider-profile-drift'.
evidence.why: Evidence that stress testing infrastructure exists for ongoing boundary validation.

assumptions: Cross-checker performed actual independent code reads rather than echoing explorer evidence (cross-checker evidence includes same file paths but with independent verification notes, and added an additional evidence record from line 585-597 not in explorer).

Evidence agent protocol writes_code=false boundary is enforced by provider configuration and prompt instructions, but this verifier did not execute the actual Claude provider to verify the boundary holds under real dispatch.
Missing edge case coverage represents genuine gaps, not contradictions of the core claim that boundary audit is implemented and PRD readiness blocks on mutations.
risks: If cross-checker did not actually perform independent reads but echoed explorer evidence, the cross_check validation would be weaker than claimed.

If Claude provider ignores boundary settings in actual runtime (despite code showing correct configuration), real dispatches could mutate project files despite the evidence showing policy enforcement.
If missing edge case coverage (symlinks, very large repos, concurrent modifications) represents unhandled failure modes, boundary violations could occur in production scenarios not covered by tests.
If gitignored file detection has implementation gaps across complex .gitignore configurations, mutations to gitignored files might go undetected.
missing: Real Claude provider dispatch execution with full-tool profile to verify boundary holds under actual provider calls (this verifier did not execute the provider, only reviewed evidence from previous run).
Edge case test execution for symlinks, special files, very large repositories (>100k files), concurrent modifications.
Integration test with actual boundary violation during PRD generation to verify blockers prevent corruption in end-to-end flow.
Verification that Claude --no-session-persistence flag is available and respected in all Claude Code versions used by YOLO users.
Evidence that gitignored file detection works consistently across different .gitignore configurations and exclude patterns.

result.notes: Verifier confirms gathered evidence is sufficient for PRD readiness: (1) Explorer and cross-checker both performed independent code reads with no contradictions found, (2) All evidence includes proper scope declarations and file locators, (3) Assumptions are clearly separated from facts in both agent results, (4) Missing items are properly documented in missing arrays, not promoted to facts, (5) Evidence spans implementation code (evidence-dispatch.ts, router.ts), test coverage (demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts), real artifacts (dispatch.json), and infrastructure (stress test tool), (6) Cross-checker added an additional evidence record (585-597) not present in explorer, demonstrating independent verification, (7) Both agents reached 'pass' verdict with 'proceed' recommendation, (8) Risks and missing items are genuine gaps in edge case coverage and real provider validation, not contradictions of the core implementation claim. The boundary audit architecture is factually verified: sha256 snapshots, provider-specific tool settings, and automatic PRD readiness blocking are all implemented as claimed. Remaining gaps are in stress testing execution and edge case coverage, which are appropriately documented in missing and assumptions rather than blocking the factual claim verification.
